case_title: ALVIN SYKES VS. DONNA BORLAND et al

docket_entries.description: SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF SYKES, ALVIN SUBSTITUTED BY MARCELLUS DAVID SYKES JR AS A PARTY TO THIS ACTION SERVED APR-21-2006, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER AS TO DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA REGENTS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE REGENTS WAS EMPLOYER. AS TO DEFENDANT DONNA BORLAND, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION (FIRST, THIRD, AND FOURTH). PLAINTIFF HAS 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 AND WILL BE HEARD ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2007 PER RECUSAL OF JUDGE BUSCH. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION. NO CLAIM WAS FILED WITH THE D.F.E.H. DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION: DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF RETALIATION. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY MAY NOT BE BROUGHT AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF STATUTORY DUTY. U.C. WAS NOT DECEDENT'S EMPLOYER. LABOR CODE SECTION 220 LIMIITS THE CITY'S LIABILITY FOR VIOLATIONS OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 203. PLAINTIFF'S THEFT CLAIM IS UNTIMELY. RULINGS ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE AS FOLLOWS: ECKMAN DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. BORLAND DECLARATION IS RULED UPON AS FOLLOWS: #1 IS OVERRULED, #2 IS OVERRULED AS TO EXHIBITS D AND F AND SUSTAINED AS TO EXHIBITS A, B, C, E AND H. #3 IS SUSTAINED. #4 IS OVERRULED. #5 IS SUSTAINED. #6 IS OVERRULED. #7 IS OVERRULED AS TO THE PIFER, KUN AND O'CONNEL DECLARATION. OBJECTION TO THE AFFILIATION AGREEMENT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR 10276

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: ALBERT M. KUN, ESQUIRE (415.362.4000) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. MARGARET W. BAUMGARTNER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY (OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3859) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. PAULA KUTANSKY-BROWN, ESQUIRE (GORDON & REES, 415.875.4238) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS BORLAND AND THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION. NO CLAIM WAS FILED WITH THE D.F.E.H. DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION: DECEDENT AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF RETALIATION. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY MAY NOT BE BROUGHT AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION BREACH OF STATUTORY DUTY. U. C. WAS NOT DECEDENT'S EMPLOYER. LABOR CODE SECTION 220 LIMIITS THE CITY'S LIABILITY FOR VIOLATIONS OF LABOR CODE SECTIONS 201 AND 203. PLAINTIFF'S THEFT CLAIM IS UNTIMELY. RULINGS ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE AS FOLLOWS: ECKMAN DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. BORLAND DECLARATION IS RULED UPON AS FOLLOWS: #1 IS OVERRULED, #2 IS OVERRULED AS TO EXHIBITS D AND F AND SUSTAINED AS TO EXHIBITS A, B, C, E AND H. #3 IS SUSTAINED. #4 IS OVERRULED. #5 IS SUSTAINED. #6 IS OVERRULED. #7 IS OVERRULED AS TO THE PIFER, KUN AND O'CONNEL DECLARATION. OBJECTION TO THE AFFILIATION AGREEMENT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR 10276

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-13-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA and DONNA BORLAND'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCE: Albert M. Kun, Esq. 362-4000 for pltf & opposing party; Michael A. Laurenson, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 986-5900 for defts & moving parties; (Judge Busch made a personal disclosure).
RULING: Argued and the Court sustained the demurrer as to deft Regents with leave to amend if plaintiff can allege Regents was employer. As to deft Borland, the demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action for retaliation, and sustained without leave to amend as to the other causes of action (first cause of action for racial discrimination, third cause of action for breach of contract, fourth cause of action for wrongful termination). Plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend. Defendant to submit proposed order. (301/PJB/DS) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
